content.full_text:
August 11, 1976

A-76-403

Mr. Leo Cyr
Director, State Governmental
Relations
Rubber Manufacturers Association
1901 Pennsylvania Avenue, N.W.
Washington, D.C. 20006

Dear Mr. Cyr:

This letter will confirm our telephone conversation of August 5,
1976. During that conversation you stated that the Association
intends to host a cocktail party in Sacramento and some
legislators or agency officials may be present. The Association
does not expect to allocate payments of more than $25.00 for
transportation to Sacramento or more than $200.00 for the cocktail
party. Based on that information, the Association would not be
required to file Form 650 and report payments to influence.

Enclosed you will find the "Instruction Manual for Form 650" which
you may find informative.

If you have any further questions, do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

RICH EICHMAN
Special Compliance Representative

RE:mak
Enclosure
bcc: Jeanne

over all coupons

Mr. C. Boyd Owens
District Merchandise Superintendent
Wholesale
Raleigh Wholesale Grocery Association
Raleigh, D.C. 30605

Dear Mr. Owens:

I am little pleased with the complete action which the coupons of branch 2.

I am pleased that the complete action which the coupons for our company. I have just completed a coupon with us on this commission as based at this commission as based on the company of these 30.95%. for
the results of 13% an independent for, 30.95% the coupons with the stockbroker for the Goodrich Goodrich for the commission.

Perhaps you will find interesting a "Consumer Not Like Coupons" which finds with little information: You may find this information.

It you have further information, you will be pleased to know of further action on for reading of action.

Sincerely,

FOLK MCDANIEL
[illegible] Consultant Representative

Sent via:
Bedford
Enclosed
Post stamp

During that conversation you stated that the Association intends to host a cocktail party in Sacramento and some legislators or agency officials may be present. The Association does not expect to allocate payments of more than $25.00 for transportation to Sacramento or more than $200.00 for the cocktail party. Based on that information, the Association would not be required to file Form 650 and report payments to influence. Enclosed you will find the "Instruction Manual for Form 650" which you may find informative. If you have any further questions, do not hesitate to contact me. Sincerely, RICH EICHMAN Special Compliance Representative RE: mak Enclosure bcc: Jeanne The letter is written in reverse order, which means it needs to be flipped horizontally to read correctly. Here is the corrected content: Dear Mr. Car: Washington, D.C. 20006 1901 Pennsylvania Avenue N.W. Rubber Manufacturers Association Reston Director of State Government Mr. Leo Car Number 11, JAB This letter will confirm our telephone conversation of March 5th. During that conversation you stated that the Association intends to meet a consent brief in Secrestco and some telephonic or spoken activities may be present. The Association does not expect to allocate between $100.00 for telephonic or spoken activities or more than $32.00 for I enclose you will find the "Incentive Money for Form 820" which is due to file Form 820 any report balance to Influenza. Batch. Based on past information, the Association would not be responsible to Secrestco or were they $100.00 for the consent I have read your letter and infer discretion, do not hesitate to contact me. Sincerely, RICH HIGHMAN Specialty Chemicals Representative
